title: auth/DoA/doa-notification-creation.spec.ts:33 › Delegated Organisation - Notification Creation › Carol Clark (agent) creates for Plant Org 1 → notification assigned to Plant Org 1 with Trade Partner badge

actorContext.evidence: trace 253df9dc actions 8-13 + snapshot before@call@517 (consignment-organisation radio list)

consignment-for.pageTitle: Who are you creating this notification for - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
consignment-for.caption: About the consignment
consignment-for.heading: Who are you creating this notification for?

options.label: IPAFFS Plant Agency C
options.note: the agent's OWN organisation (rendered first, dynamic to the signed-in agent)

options.label: A different organisation
options.note: checked in this trace — routes on to the organisation picker (consignment-organisation)

consignment-for.newSurfaceNote: This entire page never appears for a non-delegated user. It only renders when the signed-in account has DoAs. Own-org radio label is the agent's own organisation name; 'A different organisation' is the branch into the delegated-org picker.

consignment-organisation.pageTitle: Which company is this notification for - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
consignment-organisation.caption: About the consignment
consignment-organisation.heading: Which company is this notification for

fields.name: delegated-organisation
fields.note: each option renders the org name as <strong> plus its full address on separate lines; radio accessible-name concatenates name + address

consignment-organisation.newSurfaceNote: Delegated-org picker — lists every organisation the agent holds a DoA for, each with its registered trading address. The chosen org becomes the notification's responsible organisation (not the agent's own org).

autoPopulation.rule: When an agent creates for a delegated organisation, the notification's Contact details and Importer are auto-populated from that delegated organisation — the agent does not type them.

contactDetails.note: contact resolves to the delegated org's registered member (Isabel Irwin is the Plant Org 1 member)

expectedForPlantOrg1.name: IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1
expectedForPlantOrg1.address: KAINOS SOFTWARE LTD, 4-6, UPPER CRESCENT, BELFAST, BT7 1NT
expectedForPlantOrg1.country: United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland

tradePartnerBadge.evidence: trace 253df9dc snapshot before@call@... dashboard result card (snap95 refs e309-e311); assertion doa-notification-creation.spec.ts:57-58
tradePartnerBadge.location: dashboard search-result card, under the 'CHED status' term
tradePartnerBadge.rendering: two <strong> tags side by side — 'New' (status) and 'Trade Partner' (delegated-authority badge)
tradePartnerBadge.rule: A notification an agent created for a delegated (trade-partner) organisation carries a 'Trade Partner' badge on the dashboard. An own-org creation (or an org changed back to own org before submit) carries no such badge.

changeOrganisationBeforeSubmit.rule: While a notification is in Draft, the agent can amend it and change the responsible organisation. Changing from a delegated org (Plant Org 1) back to the agent's own org (IPAFFS Plant Agency C) reassigns ownership to the own org; the review page 'Save and review' button replaces 'Save and continue' when re-entering the org picker via the change link.
changeOrganisationBeforeSubmit.changeLink: #organisation-change-link on the review page (reached via Amend)

authorizationRules: confirmed/legacy: An agent account (Carol Clark, own org 'IPAFFS Plant Agency C') with DoAs can create a notification for any organisation it holds a delegated authority over. In this trace the delegated set was IPAFFS Plant Agency F, IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1, IPAFFS Plant Organisation 2.

confirmed: The notification's responsible/owning organisation is the SELECTED delegated organisation (IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1), NOT the agent's own organisation. Confirmed by review-page #responsible-organisation-name = 'IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1' and dashboard Consignee = 'IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1'.
confirmed: A delegated (agent-created) notification is flagged with a 'Trade Partner' badge on the dashboard alongside its CHED status.

legacy: A member of the responsible organisation (Isabel Irwin, IPAFFS Plant Organisation 1) creating in her own right produces a notification owned by Plant Org 1 with NO Trade Partner badge (spec test 1, doa-notification-creation.spec.ts:8-31). Implication: ownership is by responsible org regardless of author, and the badge distinguishes agent-created work from member-created work.

legacy: In Draft, the agent can change the responsible organisation (e.g. from Plant Org 1 to own org Plant Agency C) before submitting; the final ownership follows the last selection (spec test 3, doa-notification-creation.spec.ts:65-111).

legacy: Creating for a delegated org auto-populates Contact details (Isabel Irwin / ipaffs-plant-org-1-isabel@mailinator.com / 0123456789) and Importer (Plant Org 1 / KAINOS SOFTWARE LTD address) from that org, not from the agent (spec test 5 + doa.feature:42-56).
newSurface: This DoA CHED-PP journey exercises the entire delegated-agent surface the standard (single-org) journey never reaches: (1) a 'consignment-for' page ('Who are you creating this notification for?') that only renders for accounts holding DoAs, offering the agent's own org vs 'A different organisation'; (2) a 'consignment-organisation' picker ('Which company is this notification for') listing every delegated org with its full registered address; (3) auto-population of the Contact details and Importer blocks from the chosen delegated organisation rather than agent input; (4) a 'Trade Partner' badge on the dashboard result card marking agent/delegated-authored notifications; (5) an in-draft 'change organisation' link on the review page (#organisation-change-link) that lets the agent reassign the responsible org (including back to their own org) before submit. Ownership/visibility is keyed to the responsible organisation, not the authoring agent — members of the delegated org can see the notification, and the badge distinguishes it from own-org member creations.
notes: Trace is one of 5 tests in the DoA spec; only test 2 (this one) is captured in the trace. The other four tests (own-org member creation, change-org-before-submit, auto-population, and Isabel-Irwin own creation) are read from the QA spec/feature as legacy evidence for the authorization + auto-population rules. Reference number produced: CHEDPP.GB.2026.1525741. Agent's own-org radio label ('IPAFFS Plant Agency C') is hardcoded in the OrganisationSelectionPage page object, confirming it is the fixed own-org identity for this agent fixture.
